content.full_text:
State of California

Memorandum

To : Ken Finney
    Natalie West
    Mike Baker
    Bob Stern
    Nan Hambleton
    Gina Kutchins

Date : August 16, 1976

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
       Ted Prim

Subject: REPORTING FUNDRAISER PROCEEDS

       Candidate prints up tickets to a fundraiser with
a face value of $4. Candidate sells these tickets to middle-
men at a reduced price. The middlemen then sell these tickets
at their face value to persons wishing to attend the fundraiser.
Any unsold tickets may not be returned to the candidate. How
should these transactions be reported?

       If a middleman spends more than $50 purchasing
tickets from the candidate, he should be itemized as a
contributor. In addition, the campaign statement should con-
tain an explanation to the effect that the middleman has pur-
chased fundraiser tickets from the candidate for resale. The
price that the middleman paid for the tickets should be attributed
to the eventual purchaser for the purpose of determining whether
that purchaser has made $50 in contributions.


TP:pvp

120

sections.question: Candidate prints up tickets to a fundraiser with a face value of $4. Candidate sells these tickets to middlemen at a reduced price. The middlemen then sell these tickets at their face value to Persons wishing to attend the fundraiser. Any unsold tickets may not be returned to the candidate. How should these transactions be reported?
sections.conclusion: If a middleman spends more than $50 purchasing tickets from the candidate, he should be itemized as a contributor. In addition, the campaign statement should contain an explanation to the effect that the middleman has purchased fundraiser tickets from the candidate for resale. The price that the middleman paid for the tickets should be attributed to the eventual purchaser for the purpose of determining whether that purchaser has made $50 in contributions.
sections.question_synthetic: How should a candidate report fundraiser ticket sales when tickets are sold to middlemen at a discount who then resell them at face value to attendees?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Middlemen purchasing more than $50 in tickets must be itemized as contributors with an explanation of their role in ticket resale, and the discounted price they paid should be attributed to the ultimate purchaser when determining contribution thresholds.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a memorandum from FPPC staff (Ted Prim) to internal recipients. Contains clear question and conclusion sections regarding fundraiser ticket reporting under California political finance law. No formal section headers but content is clearly delineated.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Candidate prints up tickets to a fundraiser with a face value of $4. Candidate sells these tickets to middlemen at a reduced price. The middlemen then sell these tickets at their face value to Persons wishing to attend the fundraiser. Any unsold tickets may not be returned to the candidate. How should these transactions be reported?

CONCLUSION: If a middleman spends more than $50 purchasing tickets from the candidate, he should be itemized as a contributor. In addition, the campaign statement should contain an explanation to the effect that the middleman has purchased fundraiser tickets from the candidate for resale. The price that the middleman paid for the tickets should be attributed to the eventual purchaser for the purpose of determining whether that purchaser has made $50 in contributions.

Candidate sells these tickets to middle- men at a reduced price. The middlemen then sell these tickets at their face value to persons wishing to attend the fundraiser. Any unsold tickets may not be returned to the candidate. How should these transactions be reported? If a middleman spends more than $50 purchasing tickets from the candidate, he should be itemized as a contributor. In addition, the campaign statement should con- tain an explanation to the effect that the middleman has pur- chased fundraiser tickets from the candidate for resale. The price that the middleman paid for the tickets should be attributed to the eventual purchaser for the purpose of determining whether that purchaser has made $50 in contributions. TP:pvp 120
embedding.summary: FPPC advice on reporting procedures for fundraiser ticket transactions involving middlemen reselling tickets purchased from a candidate at a discount. The guidance establishes contribution reporting requirements based on the amount middlemen spend and requires price attribution to ultimate purchasers.
